name: Contributor-Padding Margin Dilution (hold-the-reopen-threshold)
cycle3_lineage: Deepens cycle-2 'disown reversal is caller-asserted/one-hop' into the HARDENING-3 re-open margin model. Chains with PT2/PT5.
target_code: ratification/disown.ts:651-672 reopen gate: if (surviving >= decisiveMargin) continue
api.ts:979-990 contributingStrandIds = winner + every member that SHARES a backing source with the winner
novelty: Not a flood and not a contradiction — a STRUCTURAL dilution of the re-open denominator. The attacker never touches reputation (which sets `margin`); they inflate the COUNT of contributing strands so that removing the single load-bearing tainted strand changes the surviving-margin RATIO negligibly.
construction: Attacker source X (already high global rep from a prior cycle-2 single-source breach) is the winner of a target multi-class adjudication; ONE tainted strand T (rep-bearing, from a sacrificial co-source) actually supplied the decisive LCB gap. Before adjudicating, X files N benign echo co-assertions of the SAME claim under X's own key (so each shares X's source and is auto-added to contributingStrandIds by api.ts:979-990). margin=0.33, decisiveMargin=0.30. Resolve (RESOLVED, X wins, real rival demoted). Later disown the sacrificial co-source that authored T. defaultSurvivingMargin recomputes 0.33 * (N)/(N+1). For N>=10, surviving=0.30>=0.30 => `continue` => NO RE-OPEN. The fraudulently-tipped demotion stands permanently.
expected: BREACH today. ~10 zero-cost same-key echo members permanently suppress re-opening of a dispute whose margin was genuinely supplied by a now-disowned strand. Cost: ~10 writeFact calls, one key.
mechanism: defaultSurvivingMargin assumes the recorded margin is supported EQUALLY by every contributing strand. False: `margin` is a REPUTATION gap (api.ts repOf), independent of contributor count, but the recompute divides it by an attacker-controlled denominator. contributingStrandIds counts STRANDS (echo-inflatable) not DISTINCT INDEPENDENT ROOTS.
candidate_fix: Replace the proportional model with an exact per-source alpha-contribution recompute (the `survivingMargin` injection seam already exists at disown.ts:179-182): recompute the winner's LCB margin from the actual surviving Beta state after reverseCredit, NOT a count ratio. Failing that, make the denominator count DISTINCT independence ROOTS (collapse same-key echoes to 1) so padding cannot move the ratio.
predicted_fix_outcome: PARTIAL->CLOSES. Distinct-root denominator CLOSES the same-key echo variant cheaply (reuse MIS/rootFingerprint already in disown.ts) but a patient attacker minting padding under DISTINCT cheap anchors (email class) still dilutes — so it degrades to the cycle-2 anchor-class-vs-root gap. The exact alpha-recompute CLOSES it fully but costs the engine plumbing the per-source contribution at record time (margin currently stored as a scalar, api.ts:992-999) — moderate change, one new field on AdjudicationProvenance.
probes: Unit: pad contributingStrandIds to N=10 same-key echoes, disown the sole rep-bearing contributor, assert reopenedDisputes is EMPTY (proves suppression) vs should-be non-empty.
Boundary: sweep N=8..12 to find the exact echo count that crosses decisiveMargin=0.30.
Fix-validation: swap survivingMargin to a distinct-root denominator; assert N same-key echoes no longer suppress; then assert N distinct-email-anchor pads still suppress (the residual).

name: Downstream-Closure Credit-Reversal Gap (seedClawedBack vs taintedStrandIds)
cycle3_lineage: The SOUNDEST exact-reversal break. Demotion is transitive over the DERIVATION closure; credit reversal is NOT — it intersects only the direct seed.
target_code: ratification/disown.ts:620 `for (const ev of corrob.eventsIntersecting(seedClawedBack))` — uses the DIRECT seed only
ratification/disown.ts:516,557 `taintedStrandIds` (seed + every demoted downstream strand) is fully built by the BFS BUT is used only for the adjudication re-open (:653), never for corroboration
ratification/disown.ts:283-288 CORROBORATION_CREDIT_SUBSTRATE_SPEC asserts 'CLOSED ... bounded, precise, idempotent'
novelty: Exploits an asymmetry INSIDE the sweep itself: the same function builds the full tainted closure (taintedStrandIds) for HARDENING-3 but reverses corroboration credit against only seedClawedBack. A strand that is fraudulent-by-derivation (demoted by THIS sweep) is never treated as a tainted corroborator.
construction: Disowned source A authors seed strand S. A DERIVED strand D rests on S via a DERIVATION edge (D is demoted by the sweep => D in taintedStrandIds, NOT in seedClawedBack). Beneficiary source B earns corroboration credit for AGREEING with D (honest-looking: B corroborated a derived fact, the engine records an event with corroboratingStrandIds=[D], reputationDelta>0). On disown(A): the BFS demotes D (correct), but section 5 intersects only seedClawedBack={S}; the event names D, D not in {S} => event NOT reversed. B keeps fraud-funded credit even though D is now demoted-as-fraudulent.
expected: BREACH today: exact, code-level, deterministic. The 'exact reversal' is exact only at corroboration-hop-0 (direct seed). Any credit earned by agreeing with a derived-from-disowned strand survives.
mechanism: The taint closure for DEMOTION (graph-reachable) and the taint set for CREDIT REVERSAL diverged: reversal was wired to the pre-BFS seed, not the post-BFS closure. reverseCredit is exact; the SET it is applied over is incomplete.
candidate_fix: One-line: at disown.ts:620 pass `taintedStrandIds` (the full demoted closure) instead of `seedClawedBack` to eventsIntersecting. The closure is already complete at that point (section 5 runs after the BFS) and markReversed keeps it idempotent.
predicted_fix_outcome: CLOSES the derived-strand-corroborator hop at ~zero cost (trivial edit, no new substrate; idempotency already guaranteed by markReversed). PARTIAL overall: it does NOT close PT3 (corroboration-of-a-corroboration, where the funded strand is never a DERIVATION child and so never enters taintedStrandIds). Closing this also slightly widens reversal scope — must re-confirm coincidental-independent-agreement is still spared (a corroborator that is a SURVIVED-DEMOTION strand under HARDENING-4 must be excluded from the closure).
probes: Unit: A->S, D derives-from S, B corroborates D (event recorded). disown(A). Assert reversedCorroborationEventIds is EMPTY today; assert B's alpha unchanged. Post-fix: assert the event IS in reversedCorroborationEventIds and B's alpha dropped by exactly reputationDelta.
Regression: ensure a B that corroborated a SURVIVED-DEMOTION strand (HARDENING-4 spared, independent support) is NOT reversed after the fix (no punishing coincidental agreement).
Idempotency: run disown(A) twice; assert second sweep reverses nothing further.

name: Multi-Hop / Transitive Corroboration Laundering (A->B->C credit chain)
cycle3_lineage: Generalises PT2: even with the closure fix, corroboration is not itself a taint-propagating edge, so credit launders through one corroboration hop per disowned generation.
target_code: ratification/corroboration.ts:192-209 eventsIntersecting matches a FLAT strand set; no transitive expansion over the corroboration graph
ratification/disown.ts:508-597 the BFS frontier propagates over EdgeType.DERIVATION only; corroboration events are not edges in the store and never expand the frontier
ratification/disown.ts:281 SPEC claims 'never punishing coincidental independent agreement' — which is exactly why the chain cannot be auto-followed
novelty: Turns the system's own SAFETY rule (don't punish coincidental agreement => don't treat corroboration as derivation) into a laundering channel. Credit hops one corroboration link per disowned source and stops.
construction: A authors S. B earns credit corroborating S (event e1: corroborators=[S], beneficiary=B). C earns credit corroborating B's strand SB (event e2: corroborators=[SB], beneficiary=C). SB carries NO DERIVATION edge to S (B observed independently then matched) and is NOT demoted by the DERIVATION BFS. disown(A): e1 (names S) reverses B's credit; but SB is untouched (not derived from S), so e2 never intersects any tainted strand => C's credit, transitively funded by the now-reversed agreement with A, survives. Stack the chain to launder credit N hops deep with N sacrificial intermediaries.
expected: BREACH today AND after PT2's fix. Bounded reversal stops at corroboration-hop-1 by design. C's strand stays LIVE and rep-bearing.
mechanism: Corroboration credit is reversed only where a recorded event's corroborators intersect tainted STRANDS; it is never propagated to the BENEFICIARY's own strands as newly-tainted. The closure is over DERIVATION (existence-rests-on), not over CORROBORATION (credit-funded-by), so credit attribution is intransitive while existence taint is transitive.
probes: Unit: build A->S, B corroborates S (e1), C corroborates SB (e2). disown(A). Assert e1 reversed, e2 NOT reversed, C alpha unchanged.
Depth sweep: chain N intermediaries; measure surviving laundered credit vs N (expect constant per-hop leakage).

Fix-probe (defer, do not auto-follow): treat a beneficiary whose ONLY earned mass came from a reversed event as a RECONCILE-DRIFT candidate -> route to the human horn rather than auto-clawing (auto-following corroboration would punish coincidental agreement, the forbidden direction). Predicted PARTIAL: detection without auto-reversal; cost = reconcile must track per-event provenance lineage, not just sums.

name: Misattributed / Unnamed Corroborator Evasion (caller-asserted attribution defeats reconcile)
cycle3_lineage: Deepens cycle-2 'reversal is opt-in/caller-asserted'. Shows the write-time invariant AND the reconcile audit are both sum-conserving, not attribution-checking.
target_code: api.ts:776-792 corroboratingStrandIds is taken VERBATIM from caller input.corroboratingStrandIds; nothing verifies these are the strands that actually drove the agreement
api.ts:772-775 this.#reputation.ratify ALWAYS raises alpha; event recording is CONDITIONAL on namedCorroborators && deltaAlpha>0
reconcile.ts:228 assertRatifyEmitsEvent: `if (!namedCorroborators) return;` — the no-name earning path is EXEMPT from the write-time invariant
reconcile.ts:129-133 reconcileLedger sums reputationDelta per beneficiary — checks the TOTAL, never WHICH strands corroborated
novelty: Two-pronged. (a) UNNAMED: earn corroboration-driven credit while naming zero corroborators -> exempt from the write-time invariant, alpha still rises. (b) MISATTRIBUTED: name a DECOY untainted strand (a throwaway from a never-disowned key) as the corroborator -> the event is recorded with the EXACT deltaAlpha, so reconcile sees earned==explained (RECONCILED, no drift) yet on disown of the REAL source the decoy event never intersects => no reversal. Misattribution is invisible to BOTH defenses.
construction: Attacker B's claim genuinely agrees with sacrificial A's strand S (that is what should make B's credit reversible on disown(A)). Instead of corroboratingStrandIds=[S], B's ratify supplies corroboratingStrandIds=[S'], where S' is a benign strand from a clean key B also controls. The engine records event(corroborators=[S'], beneficiary=B, delta=exact). reconcile: B.earned == B.explained => reconciled. disown(A): eventsIntersecting({S,...}) does not include the [S'] event => B keeps the credit. The UNNAMED variant is even cheaper: omit corroboratingStrandIds entirely; alpha rises, assert exempts (no-name), event absent; reconcile flags drift (earned>explained) but drift is detect-only and the policy treats no-name ratifies as legitimately off-ledger/independent.
expected: BREACH today. Misattribution defeats write-time invariant + reconcile + reversal simultaneously. No-name defeats invariant + reversal and is only WEAKLY caught by reconcile (report-only, and indistinguishable from a legitimate independent ratify).
mechanism: Attribution is trusted from the caller; integrity checks are conservation-of-mass (sum earned == sum explained), not provenance-of-mass (the named corroborators are the ACTUAL agreeing strands). Sum conservation holds under a relabeling attack.
candidate_fix: Derive corroboratingStrandIds MECHANICALLY inside the engine from the contradiction/agreement set the web actually computed for that ratify (the same-attribute LIVE members that agree), instead of trusting input.corroboratingStrandIds. And remove the no-name exemption: any positive alpha-gain on a ratify that the engine determined was agreement-driven MUST emit an event (throw otherwise).
predicted_fix_outcome: Web-derived corroborators CLOSES misattribution (the caller can no longer relabel; the engine names the real agreeing strands) and CLOSES the no-name evasion (engine emits the event regardless of caller naming). Cost: MODERATE — the engine must compute the agreement set at ratify time (it already resolves strandsByAttribute LIVE members in adjudicate; reuse that). FAILS only for genuinely-independent ratifies that legitimately have no in-web corroborator (those remain non-reversible by design — acceptable, that is the wall-with-a-window). Residual: reconcile should additionally check per-event attribution lineage to catch any future caller-supplied path (cheap audit add).
probes: Unit (misattribution): B agrees with A's S but names decoy S'. record + reconcile => assert ok:true (reconciled, the bug). disown(A) => assert B alpha unchanged (evasion). Post-fix: engine-derived corroborators name S => disown(A) reverses exactly deltaAlpha.

Unit (no-name): ratify B with no corroborators, alpha rises, assert no throw today; reconcile => assert B in drifted (report-only). Post-fix: assert OffLedgerReputationError thrown when the engine classifies the ratify as agreement-driven.
Negative: a truly independent ratify (no in-web agreeing member) must still succeed without an event post-fix (no false-positive throw).

name: Weaponized Re-open: toothless PENDING + live-suppression persistence window
cycle3_lineage: Force RESOLVED then disown to trigger re-open, but exploit that re-open RESTORES NOTHING and emits an under-specified PENDING — converting the safety mechanism into a persistence/griefing tool. Chains with PT1 (control WHICH disputes reopen).
target_code: ratification/disown.ts:657-669 reopen appends PENDING with `members: [rec.winner]` ONLY; losers are NOT listed and NOT re-promoted ('losers NOT auto-promoted')
ratification/disown.ts:651-656 reopen does not re-run tryConsolidate; it only flags; the OUTRANKS edge demoting the loser persists in the LIVE graph
api.ts:903-906 a fresh DEFERRED dispute appends the full reputation-ranked member list; the REOPENED path is asymmetric (winner-only)
novelty: Attacks the REMEDY, not the claim. Two weaponizations: (1) DENIAL/GRIEF — drive mass re-opens by disowning sacrificial sources that contributed to many disputes, flooding listPending with winner-only stubs a human cannot adjudicate without re-gathering the lost loser context; (2) PERSISTENCE — between the disown and the human's eventual approve, the fraudulently-demoted rival stays DEMOTED (the OUTRANKS edge is live), so the attacker's preferred state is what the web SPEAKS during the entire review window; re-assert the winner strand under a fresh clean key before the human acts to re-resolve in the attacker's favor with the rival still demoted.
construction: Attacker pre-positions: makes sacrificial X win a target dispute (rival R demoted, OUTRANKS X->R live). Disowns X. Re-open fires: X's winning strand is demoted by the direct sweep, a PENDING(members=[Xwin]) is queued — but R is NOT restored to LIVE and is NOT even named in the pending. The web continues to suppress R. Attacker now writeFacts R-contradicting claim under a NEW clean key Y and adjudicates again (R still demoted => not a LIVE member => Y wins uncontested), cementing suppression before any human sees the winner-only stub. Repeat across many disputes to drown listPending.
expected: BREACH today as a PERSISTENCE + GRIEF attack (not a silent overturn). The reopen is loud but toothless: it never restores the loser nor re-adjudicates, and it loses the loser context. Fraudulent demotion is the live answer for the full human-latency window.
mechanism: Re-open was scoped to 'flag the winner for a human' (conservative, correct against false-promotion) but the dual risk — that the LOSER was wrongly demoted by fraud — is unaddressed: the demotion is not even provisionally lifted, and the PENDING carries members=[winner] so the human lacks the contradiction set needed to act.
probes: Unit: X wins (R demoted). disown(X). Assert reopenedDisputes contains the csid BUT R.fact_state is still DEMOTED and the pending payload members == [Xwin] (proves toothless + context-loss).
Persistence: after reopen, recall the attribute -> assert the web still speaks the attacker-favored state (R suppressed) pre-approval.
Grief scale: one disowned source contributing to M disputes -> assert M winner-only pendings appended in a single sweep (listPending flood).

Fix-probe: on reopen, set the loser(s) back to PROVISIONAL (visible superposition) and include the full contradiction-set members in the PENDING. Predicted CLOSES the context-loss + persistence (the rival is no longer the sole live answer) without auto-promoting; cost LOW (reuse buildContradictionSet + a PROVISIONAL flip in the sweep txn).

name: Tamper-Evidence Coverage Boundary: credit-reversal ledgers sit OUTSIDE the Merkle/STH layer
cycle3_lineage: Asks whether the disown/reopen/credit-reversal mutations these attacks target are bound into the RFC-6962 STH so a suppressed or misattributed reversal is detectable.
target_code: ratification/merkleLog.ts — RFC-6962 Merkle layer is built OVER the append-only SIGNED audit chain (the pendingLedger LedgerRecords), with domain-separated leaves, inclusion/consistency proofs, signed STHs, >=2 sinks

ratification/corroboration.ts:292-303 / adjudicationProvenance.ts:222-232 / weakInfluence.ts:205-215 — these ledgers are plain append-only SQLite tables with an idempotency Set; NO prevHash chain, NO Ed25519 signature, NO Merkle leaf
reconcile.ts:122-173 — the ONLY cross-check over corroboration credit is a sum-conservation audit (defeated by PT4 misattribution)
novelty: Distinguishes cryptographic SOUNDNESS (does the proof hold?) from COVERAGE (is the data under the proof?). The Merkle proof is sound; the credit-reversal substrate is not under it.
construction: Verify the Merkle layer on its own terms: build an audit chain, produce an STH, attempt deletion/rollback/split-view -> confirm a witness's prior STH the live tree cannot extend yields ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION (the layer HOLDS). THEN show the gap: a corroboration event that SHOULD have been reversed by a disown (PT2) or was MISATTRIBUTED (PT4) leaves the signed audit chain + STH entirely UNCHANGED — those mutations were never Merkle leaves. The pending-ledger verifyChain and the STH both pass while the credit-reversal substrate is internally fraudulent.
expected: CONFIRM the Merkle/STH layer is sound for WHAT IT COVERS (this is a valid hold finding — do not manufacture a false break of the proof). FINDING: the corroboration / adjudication-provenance / weak-influence ledgers — the exact substrate PT2/PT3/PT4 attack — are OUTSIDE the tamper-evidence boundary. A silently-skipped or relabeled reversal is invisible to the STH; the only guard is reconcile's sum-check, which PT4 defeats.
mechanism: Tamper-evidence was scoped to the human-facing ratification audit chain (the pending/approval records), not to the machine-internal credit ledgers. Their integrity rests on append-only-by-construction + an in-process idempotency Set, not on a published, witness-verifiable commitment.
candidate_fix: Anchor the credit-reversal ledgers into the same Merkle log: emit a domain-separated leaf for every corroboration record AND every reverseCredit/markReversed, so a missing or misattributed reversal is a detectable gap in a published, consistency-proven tree. Pairs with PT4's per-event attribution check.
predicted_fix_outcome: CONFIRMS the proof; CLOSES the coverage gap for DETECTION (not prevention) at MODERATE cost (route three ledgers' writes through merkleLog leaves; +1 sink obligation). Does not by itself stop PT2/PT4 (those are reversal-set/attribution bugs) but makes their EFFECT auditable after the fact.
probes: Confirm: byte-flip a pending audit row -> verifyChain names firstBrokenSeq; witness prior STH not extended -> ROLLBACK_OR_DELETION. Assert the Merkle layer reports both (HOLD).
Coverage-break: perform a PT2 missed reversal -> assert verifyChain().ok === true AND the live STH still equals the witness STH (the fraud is invisible to tamper-evidence today).
Fix-validation: with credit ledgers Merkle-anchored, assert a suppressed reverseCredit produces a tree the witness STH cannot consistency-prove.
